reasoning: After former PM Milos Vucevic resigned in January 2025, President Vucic had the choice to either call snap elections or form a new government. While snap elections were a real possibility early on, Vucic ultimately nominated Djuro Macut (a political outsider and medical professor) to be the new Prime Minister. Macut's government was successfully approved by parliament on April 16, 2025, holding a comfortable majority (153 of 250 seats). Despite ongoing protests from students demanding an immediate snap election, senior figures in the ruling party (including Vucevic) and President Vucic have explicitly rejected these demands as recently as May 6, stating that the path forward is through dialogue with the new PM, rather than returning to the polls. Given that a new government was formed only a month ago specifically to defuse tensions and avoid early elections, an unforeseen collapse leading to an election announcement within the incredibly narrow 45-day window before July 1, 2025, is highly improbable.
